sessions.title: Hook up LLM mock summarizer for session repack
prompts: session repack feature. we have an llm mock summarizer. can we hook it up? I want a starting prompt: "Summarize the activities and interactions for an executive asking a few questions: What did I used
[Request interrupted by user]
resume
yes. add small edit button next to LLM summarizer button to open the prompt for edits if needed.
sessions.outcome: Good. The changes are clean. Here's what's wired up: **`summarize.js`** - `DEFAULT_REPORT_SYSTEM` — the executive prompt, exported so it can be edited/overridden - `summarizeDayReport(day, provider, systemPrompt)` — aggregates all interactive sessions, calls provider, stores `day.report` + `day.reportAt` + `day.reportMeta` **`index.js`** - `getConfig()` / `saveConfig()` — persist the custom prompt

sessions.title: Assess markitdown feature and port to different server
prompts: assess the markitdown feature and determine the bests mechanism to port it to a different server. include option for python executable.
is there an option that wont require a node server? or even python?
no I want to keep using markitdown. I was hoping we could package as a self contained executable.
does option a retain the ui?
what would a user have to do if I shared it
is there a way to port the ui to a python native form and ship with exe? all self contained?
could the ui offer a fallback to launch in chrome?
so skip the python native form, and just launch chrome with the exe. sounds simpler that way. how hard is it to edit the app with just the exe?
yes
[Request interrupted by user]
resume
will you build it?
commands: cd C:/Sandbox && git add .gitignore package.json apps/markitdown/service/main.py apps/markitdown/service/ui.html apps/ma
cd C:/Sandbox && git push origin main
sessions.outcome: That's the launcher checking for the venv at startup. Two paths depending on what's on that server: **Python is available there:** ``` npm run markitdown:setup ``` That's it — creates the venv and installs markitdown. One-time, then it auto-starts. **No Python on that server:** Set env vars to point the Node app at the Python service running somewhere else: ``` MARKITDOWN_LAUNCH=external MARKITDOW

sessions.title: Write prompt for port catalog CLI routing
prompts: look at port catalog -> route through cli seam write the prompt. first variant is for node server. claude-engine does not exist on the target server. avoid specific references or links that appear on
is port available on github
yes
add an entry for markitdown core app. Assume UI is to be kept as is.
sessions.outcome: Done. Entry is live at [jdevins/sandbox](https://github.com/jdevins/sandbox). The prompt is structured in four steps: 1. **policy.json** — the JSON verbatim, since it's the source of truth for both layers 2. **Python service** — `main.py` contract (endpoints, tempfile pattern, .csv fallback, shutdown hook, standalone entry point) + `requirements.txt` 3. **Node lib files** — `env.js`, `launcher.js`
